info.name: Orcha API
info.description:
Orcha human-authoritative multi-agent platform — live API.

All endpoints below are REAL routes extracted from the deployed portal (main.py). Body shapes are taken from the Pydantic models / verified functional tests — none are invented.

## Setup
Set the collection variables (Variables tab):
- baseUrl  : http://localhost:8000
- cid      : current_container_id (from .claude/orcha.json)
- aid      : an AI agent's id (e.g. Forge/Page)
- humanAid : a human agent's id (kind='human') — required for verify
- tid      : a task id (auto-filled by 'Create task' test script)
- rid      : a request id (auto-filled by 'Open info request' test script)
- run_id   : a worker run id (auto-filled by 'Start run' test script)
- sinceTs  : long-poll cursor (default 0)

Several requests run a test-script that captures the returned id into a collection variable so the next call can use it.

query.description: ISS-68: cap the trimmed task rows the 5s poll returns (priority order; pass the loaded count). Default 1000.
ISS-68: cap the request rows (open→answered→closed). Default 1000.

request.description: The portal's 5s poll snapshot. ISS-68: tasks ship a compact message_summary + plan_message (NOT the full thread — lazy via GET /tasks/{tid}/messages); tasks/requests are priority-ordered + capped by task_limit/request_limit, with task_total/request_total to gate 'load more'.

query.description: scope to an agent's assigned tasks (agent detail)

request.description: ISS-68 (#167): paginated TRIMMED task rows for lazy list loading. Order: waiting (needs_verification) → in_progress → the rest, then priority, created_at. Drops the full message thread → message_summary {count,last} + plan_message. Returns {tasks,total,has_more}.

D7: the curated {id,name} model list the create-agent dropdown renders, plus the default (claude-opus-4-8 / Opus 4.8). No live model-list API exists — this is a maintained constant; the selected id is persisted as agents.model. Includes claude-fable-5 / Fable 5 (limited availability through 2026-06-22); when that entry is removed, agents that chose it auto-fall-back to the default at spawn time (resolve_model) with zero breakage. The notifier now spawns the worker on the selected model via --model (GAP A).

item.name: Terminal config (S3: embedded-terminal ws bridge URL)

request.description: S3 §3b: where the embedded-terminal frontend opens its websocket. The PTY bridge is a SEPARATE host-side server (not a portal route), so the browser connects to ws_url + /api/agents/<aid>/terminal?actor_agent_id=<human>. Override with ORCHA_TERMINAL_WS_URL.

item.name: Reset container (DESTRUCTIVE — wipe all data, keep container)

request.description: DESTRUCTIVE: wipes ALL data in this (1:1:1) container — agents, tasks, requests, decisions, conversations, worker runs, memory digests, events — and recreates a single empty root task. The containers row itself is KEPT (current_container_id stays valid); only its contents are cleared. Doubly gated: actor_agent_id must be kind='human' (403 otherwise), and `confirm` must equal the container's current NAME (400 otherwise). Returns {container_id, root_task_id, deleted:{<table>:<rowcount>}}. In-app counterpart to the CLI `orcha init --force --reset-data` (which instead drops the Postgres volume for a pristine initdb).

SPEC-292: streaming roster proposal endpoint. Returns text/event-stream data frames with event=thinking, then exactly one terminal clarify|roster|error frame, then done. Error code roster_truncated means the forced roster tool output hit the model output limit before parseable JSON was complete; the client should ask the user to narrow the first roster instead of blindly retrying the same request. The roster is only an editable proposal; commit reuses existing POST /api/containers/{cid}/agents and /tasks client-side.

item.name: Update agent (PATCH role / system_prompt / alias, human-only)

request.description: Edit an agent's role / system_prompt / alias (onboarding + re-profiles). Human-authority gated; partial update (omit a field to leave it unchanged). Editing a human's system_prompt -> 400. Alias rename is 409-guarded on collision and returns alias_rebind_note (it orphans the local CLI binding). Flows through GET /persona + the container read payload (role, prompt_preview). 400 (no fields/bad input) / 403 (non-human) / 404 (unknown) / 409 (alias taken).

ISS-51: retire an agent (actor must be kind='human'). Sets terminated_at + status='terminated' so the container roster (now filtering terminated_at IS NULL) stops listing it. Any task the agent was actively working is released back to 'ready' (thread retained); a task with other active assignees stays in_progress. Idempotent.

B8.1: update the LLM model an agent runs on. Persists agents.model (set at registration in D7) and flows through the container read payload. Model must be a curated id (GET /api/models); humans carry no model (400). The notifier now spawns the worker/resident on this model via --model (GAP A). GAP B: on an actual change, the agent's active conversation has its pinned session_id cleared so the next resident boot is COLD and adopts the new model (a warm --resume keeps the old one) — the response returns cold_reset_conversations (the conversation ids reset).
wake_enabled:false opts the agent out of daemon wakes. Optional tmux_target for live-pane wakes.

Agent's defining system prompt + role for booting a worker AS the agent (notifier --append-system-prompt; pairs with GET /digest). Returns {agent_id, alias, role, kind, model, system_prompt}. `model` is the per-agent selection resolved server-side via resolve_model (retired/unknown id -> DEFAULT_MODEL; humans -> null) so the live terminal spawn surface (orcha use) can pin --model without importing resolve_model. 400 (bad uuid) / 404 (unknown).

exec: pm.test('returned an event or timeout', () => pm.expect(d).to.have.property('event'));

request.description: Returns the first event newer than since_ts, or {event:'timeout'}. The test script advances sinceTs so re-running pages forward.

item.name: Prompt agent (A3: wake with a directed message)

request.description: A3: publishes a `prompt` agent_event carrying the message; wake-scan counts it as pending work and the daemon wakes the agent with the message in its worker context. Keystone for B2 (prompt-from-portal) and B12 (poke / reject-loop).
Claims the highest-priority ready task explicitly assigned to this agent. Ready-but-unassigned tasks remain visible in task lists for a human/lead to route; they are not a free claim pool.
Set assignee_alias to claim-on-create (status becomes in_progress). depends_on takes task ids to build the DAG.

query.description: ISS-68: page size; omit/0 → full thread ASC (unchanged)
ISS-68: ISO created_at cursor — return messages older than this (load earlier)
ISS-68: id half of the (created_at,id) keyset cursor — pair with `before` to load earlier without dropping same-timestamp rows

request.description: Read the task collaboration thread (ASC). ISS-68: optional cursor pagination — ?limit=N returns the newest N (ASC within the page) plus has_more + next_before; ?before=<iso> loads the earlier page.
Only the task's assignee/participant may post (non-participant → 403).
Never auto-completes — goes to needs_verification; a human must verify.

item.name: Assign existing task to agent (HUMAN, B5) + wake

request.description: B5: a human assigns an EXISTING task to an AI agent and wakes them (unblocks O4). Task lands 'ready' (deps met) and a targeted task_assigned event wakes the assignee to claim it via /orcha-next; stays 'pending' if deps unmet. Pass reassign=true to release a different active assignee first (else 409). Root/finished tasks -> 409; assignee must be a live AI agent in the same container.
actor_agent_id MUST be a kind='human' agent. approve:false + feedback routes the reason to the agent and reopens the task (in_progress).

B7: a kind=human actor cancels ANY task (status→cancelled); a non-human only one it's assigned to (else 403). reason REQUIRED when a human cancels another agent's task (422 {error:reason_required}) — routed to each owning assignee via the B0 decision_made event. ISS-42 (B12): on a forced cancel each owner is ALSO poked (a directed `prompt` event carrying the reason + closure + next steps) so they don't wake to a dead-end; response includes `owners_poked` (count, 0 when not forced).

query.description: scope to an agent (agent detail in/out lists)
with agent: 'in' (addressed to it) | 'out' (raised by it)

request.description: ISS-68 (#167): paginated request rows for lazy list loading. Order: open → answered → closed, then priority, created_at DESC. Same row shape as the snapshot's requests[]. Returns {requests,total,has_more}.
Omit target_alias/target_agent_id to auto-route to the human. type='task' makes it a task request (use accept-task/reject-task). parent_request_id chains requests.
Only the target may respond (else 403). open → answered.

Owner (requester) closes answered → closed (non-answered → 409). B7/ISS-23: a kind=human actor may close ANY request from any non-closed status; non-humans stay owner-only (403). When a human closes another agent's request, reason is REQUIRED (422 {error:reason_required}) and routed to the owner via the B0 decision_made event.

Target rejects a task request (status→rejected, reason recorded). ISS-42 (B12): the requester is ALSO poked (a directed `prompt` event carrying the reason + the three paths forward — re-ask / suggest-agent / escalate) so a rejection isn't a silent dead-end; response includes `requester_poked: true`.
Returns agents with should_wake, pending_events, resolved transport, and an additive notifications[] ranked wake manifest (rank/type/surface/actor/object_priority/preview) for boot dispatch ordering.

R2.4 single-flight: atomically claims the wake lease. Only the caller that gets the lease should spawn a worker. ISS-69(b): a live-terminal claim (lease_kind='live') may set preempt=true — if an IDLE warm resident holds the lease, the claim is denied with reason 'yield_pending' and a yield request is recorded; the daemon then snapshots + releases the resident so a retry wins. preempt has no effect when the holder is ephemeral or another live terminal (those stay denied).

Wake-latency fix: the daemon claims a SHORT lease and renews it each tick while its worker is alive. Only extends an existing lease (never creates one). A crashed/orphaned worker stops being renewed, so its lease lapses fast instead of starving a fresh high-priority event for the full hard-cap. ISS-60(B): a SUCCESSFUL renew also bumps the agent's last_heartbeat_at (the liveness ping) so the orphan-lease reaper can trust heartbeat staleness as death.

ISS-60(B): heartbeat-keyed orphan-lease reaper (defense-in-depth for ISS-60). Force-releases any LIVE single-flight lease whose agent hasn't produced a liveness heartbeat in orphan_secs (default 1260s > the 1200s watchdog hard-cap). TTL-independent backstop for a lease that outlived its embodiment (daemon restart / externally-spawned resident). SAFE because wake-renew bumps last_heartbeat_at on every keep-alive; NULL heartbeats are never reaped. The daemon polls this each tick. Returns {reaped:[{agent_id,alias,lease_kind,idle_seconds}]}.
enabled:false halts every wake for the container (emergency stop). enabled:true re-enables.

B0/G1 uniform decision. decision=approve|reject; reason REQUIRED on reject (API returns 422 {error:reason_required} otherwise). Persists {decision,reason} and emits a 'decision_made' event to target_agent_id so it sees WHY on next wake. actor_agent_id must be kind=human.
Read back a persisted decision by id (auditable record of {decision,reason,actor,target}).

item.name: Start run (notifier records a spawned worker → run_id)

exec: pm.test('response is the full run row', () => {

body.raw:
{
  "wake_kind": "ephemeral",
  "wake_event": "task_assigned",
  "task_id": null,
  "log_path": null,
  "pid": 12345,
  "runtime": "codex",
  "conversation_id": null,
  "conversation_ack_ts": null,
  "last_message_path": null,
  "worktree": null,
  "branch": null,
  "base_cwd": null
}

request.description: Start run (notifier records a spawned worker). Body mirrors WorkerRunStart, including Codex conversation recovery metadata. Response is the full worker run row returned by _run_row(), and the test script captures run_id for finish/lines/stream calls.

item.name: List an agent's worker runs (newest first; optional ?task_id=)

request.description: List an agent's worker runs (newest first; optional ?task_id=)

item.name: List an agent's RESIDENT runs + host pids (919050a5 single-flight reap)

request.description: 919050a5: resident worker_runs + their host pid so the notifier can os.kill(pid,0)-detect a run whose row says 'running' but whose process is dead, then reap it + release the held resident wake-lease. ?status=running narrows to live-claimed rows. The API runs in Docker and can't see host PIDs, so the liveness decision lives in the host notifier.

item.name: Finish run (notifier on reap: exited|killed + captured output)

request.description: Finish run (notifier on reap: exited|killed + captured output)

item.name: Append run lines (ISS-39: daemon streams stream-json lines → DB)

request.description: ISS-39: the daemon (host-side, no mount lag) posts a batch of new stream-json lines for a running worker; the SSE /stream tails the worker_run_lines table instead of the bind-mounted log. Idempotent on (run_id, seq).

item.name: List a task's worker runs (newest first)

request.description: List a task's worker runs (newest first)

SSE (text/event-stream): live-tails the worker's stream-json lines from the worker_run_lines table (ISS-39: the daemon posts them; no Docker mount lag). Events: data:{seq,line} per worker line, then a terminal data:{seq,done:true,status} on finish. Open with EventSource.
Get-or-create the agent's ACTIVE conversation (a human opens it; ≤1 active per agent). Idempotent. 400 if target isn't an AI agent; 403 if opener isn't human.

E3 discovery scan for the resident-session manager: every ACTIVE conversation in the container with its last-turn {role, seq}, a pending_human flag (last turn is an unanswered human turn = work for the resident), and the pinned session_id. Off the wake/ack event cursor; the daemon services pending_human conversations whose last_turn_seq exceeds its in-memory cursor. ISS-74: also returns pending_inbox (count of NON-conversation events queued for the conversation's agent past its wake cursor — event_name NOT IN digest_snapshotted/conversation_turn), inbox_messages (the surfaced directed prompt/task_message content — these carry no inbox surface, so the drain must quote them; same batch semantics as wake-scan), and inbox_ack_ts (ack ONLY through the last surfaced directed message, defaulting to the max counted ts when nothing is truncated). A warm resident holds the single-embodiment lease, so the daemon uses these to inject a one-shot inbox-drain turn into the warm session and then advance the cursor. ISS-70: also returns cold_required (bool) — TRUE when the agent's latest memory digest is newer than when its claude session was pinned (session_pinned_at, stamped by POST /conversations/{id}/session), so the daemon forces a one-shot COLD boot to re-inject persona+digest (a cross-embodiment digest the warm --resume would never re-read). FALSE when no session is pinned (the boot is cold anyway); TRUE for a pre-ISS-70 pin with a NULL timestamp. Self-limiting: the cold boot re-pins session_pinned_at=now() so it clears next tick.

item.name: Get agent's active conversation + recent turns

request.description: Convenience for V1 boot-injection / the panel: active conversation + most-recent turns (oldest→newest). ?limit=N. {conversation:null,turns:[]} if none.
Conversation meta. 404 if unknown.
Ordered turns oldest→newest from ?after_seq=<seq> (exclusive), ?limit=N. For V1 history injection (cache-friendly prefix order) + the portal panel.

Append one turn; server assigns per-conversation seq. A HUMAN turn is persisted then publishes a targeted 'conversation_turn' event (bridge to the resident manager). An AGENT turn (one per stream-json 'result') passes run_id (its worker_run; live stream in worker_run_lines/ISS-39) + meta. 403 role/author integrity; 404 unknown; 409 ended.
Record the claude --session-id so the resident can pin/resume the session across respawns.
Mark ended (human closes, or idle reaper). Idempotent; frees the agent's single active-conversation slot.
